items.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby

items.title: Iran pushes back against Trump's deadline

items.title: Middle East war live: Iran Revolutionary Guards’ intelligence chief Khademi killed in strikes
items.standfirst: The head of ‌the Revolutionary Guards’ intelligence ‌organisation, Majid Khademi, was on Monday killed in US-Israeli air strikes. Khademi “was martyred in the criminal terrorist attack by the American-Zionist enemy ... at dawn today", the Guards announced in a post on their Telegram channel. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

clusters.title: Explosives found by pipeline to Hungary: PM Orban clams sabotage
clusters.neutral_headline: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
bullet_summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban has suggested explosives found near a pipeline supplying Russian gas to Hungary and Serbia was "an act of sabotage"
Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: Explosives found by pipeline to Hungary: PM Orban clams sabotage
items.standfirst: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said the army and police had found two backpacks containing explosives Sunday near a gas pipeline to Hungary, prompting Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban to call an emergency meeting.
items.content: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said the army and police had found two backpacks containing explosives Sunday near a gas pipeline to Hungary, prompting Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban to call an emergency meeting.

items.title: Explosives discovered near gas pipeline to Hungary, says Serbia's president
items.standfirst: Powerful explosives were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline in Serbia that carries Russian gas to Hungary, the leaders of the two countries said on Sunday, triggering political scrutiny in Hungary days before a national election.
items.content: Powerful explosives were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline in Serbia that carries Russian gas to Hungary, the leaders of the two countries said on Sunday, triggering political scrutiny in Hungary days before a national election.

items.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
items.standfirst: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.
items.content: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

items.title: Hungarian PM faces ‘false flag’ claims after Serbia says explosives found near pipeline
items.standfirst: Incident prompts political scrutiny across Hungary as Viktor Orbán trails in polls before next Sunday’s electionSerbia has said it found “explosives of devastating power” near a pipeline that carries Russian natural gas to Hungary and beyond, sparking claims by Hungary’s leading opposition candidate of a possible “false flag” operation aimed at influencing the country’s elections.On Sunday, Hungary’s prime minister, Viktor Orbán, said he had been informed by Serbia’s president, Aleksandar Vučić, of the discovery near an extension of the TurkStream pipeline, which transports Russian gas through the Balkans to central and eastern Europe. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Set to Fly by Far Side of the Moon
clusters.neutral_headline: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Set to Fly by Far Side of the Moon
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, NPR News

items.title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Set to Fly by Far Side of the Moon
items.standfirst: A NASA crew of four astronauts will fly by the moon on Monday at the closest distance they will get to the lunar surface during their mission and the nearest any human has gotten to the moon in more than 50 years.
items.content: A NASA crew of four astronauts will fly by the moon on Monday at the closest distance they will get to the lunar surface during their mission and the nearest any human has gotten to the moon in more than 50 years.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II crew readies for Monday's lunar flyby. Here's what you need to know
items.standfirst: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.
items.content: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)

items.title: New UK farm inheritance tax rule will cause ‘significant challenges’, say accountants
items.standfirst: Levy on inherited farms and family businesses worth £2.5m or more comes into force 6 AprilA new inheritance tax regime for UK farms and family businesses comes into force on Monday and will present “significant challenges” for those affected, according to accountants.In October 2024 the government announced plans to levy inheritance tax on farms – prompting an outcry in many quarters.
